action.reasoning: Observation: Current best LPIPS=0.6021 (iter16, partial orbit fallback); recent custom rays with offset=0 give ~0.61-0.62 + matmul errors (7680x64 vs 120x64) on some offsets/objs in val_eval (likely from rays.view(-1,3) breaking expected [1,H,W,3] shape to renderer + missing triplane extraction + no elev clamp causing pole degeneracies). Workspace has near-complete robust funcs (get_spherical_cameras_with_azimuth with eps/alt_up, get_rgb_from_output, tensor_to_rgba_pil, compute_metrics) but truncated mains + best_offset.txt=0. History confirms inspect validated renderer(model.decoder, triplane, rays_o, rays_d) + rel_az for [1,256,256,3] output. Analysis: Pose mismatch (dataset rel_az ~185° avg, varying input_az) is root cause of >0.6 LPIPS; offset=180 + elev clamp [-85,85] + keepdim rays (no flatten) + triplane=scene_codes['triplane'] if dict else scene_codes + white-BG composite + chunk=16384 + per-obj gc/empty_cache will fix errors and yield lower LPIPS (expected <0.55). Zero-shot TripoSR+per-target alignment optimal (<1B params, no training, fits 2 iters/7h left). Decision: Overwrite val_eval.py+predict.py with COMPLETE self-contained corrected code (full mains, offset=180 default + load from txt, elev clamp, no ray flatten, robust triplane extract, cleanup, error resilience, meets eval contract exactly); run val_eval.py (limit=20 objs for speed) to confirm improved val LPIPS + update best_offset.txt; if successful submit_eval on best.pt + predict.py.
action.command: python val_eval.py && echo 'Val LPIPS test completed with best offset' && cat best_offset.txt
